rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WILSON vs MAXXAM, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings; Req. For Judicial Ntc; Compendium Of Non-California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 3, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANTS CHARLES HURWITZ AND MAXXAM INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the documents requested by defendants. Although defendants insist that the facts supporting the alleged fraud are disclosed in Exhibits A and B, defendants do not point to specific pages in the lengthy exhibit that sets forth the facts upon which defendants rely. The Court is inclined to grant judgment on the pleadings based on the decision in People ex rel. Gallegos v. Pacific Lumber Co. (2008) 158 Cal.Ap.4th 950. That decision holds that the Noerr-Pennington doctrine is applicable to defendants' submission of the Sustained Yield Plan ("SPY") to the State. Gallegos addressed another part of the plan, but in Gallegos, defendants also were charged with using improper tactics to obtain permission to harvest certain levels of timber. Plaintiffs contend that a false claims act cause of action is fundamentally different from an unfair competition claim because the false claims act involves the public fisc. The argument is not supported by case law. Moreover, the Noerr-Pennington doctrine promotes vigorous advocacy to governmental bodies; it is based on principles similar to Civil Code section 47 that protects virtually all forms of conduct in the context of litigation. The Court does not find plaintiffs' distinction to be persuasive. Nor is the Court concerned that such a ruling would undermine the false claims act. Although the Court rejected the contention that plaintiffs had not plead a false claim, the ruling was based on a demurrer and the claim alleged is far from a garden variety false claim made to a governmental entity. =(302/PJM/PB)
